description.value:
Trigger A creature activates a device with a screen aboard Moonside-250

Effect A silhouette with a single, glowing white eye takes shape in the static and probes the triggering creature's mind. The target must attempt a @Check[will|dc:26] save. Regardless of the result, the creature experiences an intrusive thought: "Stay here forever."

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature feels eyes watching them and panics as the eerie gaze seems to bore into their flesh. The creature takes @Damage[(1d8+2)[mental]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature feels their deepest self being perceived. Ask the creature a question; the first time a creature fails against this haunt, the question is "What is the worst thing to ever happen to you?" Future questions build off this topic, such as "How old were you?", "Did you deserve it?", or "Do you dream of it?" The creature must answer honestly but doesn't have to respond out loud. The creature takes @Damage[(2d8+4)[mental]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature feels their sense of self fading as they gaze at the image in the static. The creature is asked three questions, takes @Damage[(4d8+8)[mental]] damage, is frightened 2 and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Fleeing] for 3 rounds, and is exposed to the Watcher's Stare curse.

details.description: The screen erupts into static shaped vaguely like the outline of a person with only half a head.
